summary: The draft is source-traceable and technically rich, but it is too broad for a single Ready story and assigns a mixed database/API/frontend/worker/reporting scope to one executor. It should be split or narrowed before implementation.

findings: No obvious unresolved template variables remain in core story sections.
Dev Agent Record and QA Results are appropriately placeholder-only for a draft.
The story includes a Story Draft Checklist Result, but the result itself says NEEDS PO VALIDATION.
The assignment fits the database/RLS/migration portion, but the story also includes substantial frontend, API/proxy, async worker, reports, exports, and anomaly alert work.

critical_issues.title: Story scope is too large for Ready
critical_issues.detail: The draft combines Finance visual shell, 14-table data model, RLS, receivables, payables, cost centers, P&L, reconciliation workers, forecast, calendar, audit, reports, exports, anomaly alerts, Arva slots, cross-module events, and broad test coverage. This is effectively a multi-sprint epic slice, not a single executable story.
critical_issues.recommendation: Split into child stories aligned to the documented sprints or technical surfaces: database/RLS baseline, receivables/dashboard, payables/cost centers/P&L, reconciliation worker/API/UI, forecast/calendar, audit/reports/anomalies, and Finance visual/Arva shell.

critical_issues.title: Executor assignment does not cover the full work type
critical_issues.detail: @data-engineer is appropriate for schema/RLS/migrations, but the draft requires major frontend, Next.js proxy, service/worker, UI workflow, reports/export, and integration implementation.
critical_issues.recommendation: Either narrow this story to the data-engineering slice or create separate stories with executor assignments such as @dev for API/frontend logic and @ux-design-expert for design-heavy UI if the framework requires it.

should_fix_issues.title: Backend/Supabase path decision remains unresolved
should_fix_issues.detail: The story notes that product docs reference backend/FastAPI/Supabase paths not present in the repo, while current repo architecture starts under apps/portal and packages/*. This affects migration and worker tasks.
should_fix_issues.recommendation: Before Ready, make the implementation boundary explicit for the first slice: create tracked backend/supabase surfaces with workspace integration, or limit this story to portal/proxy/contracts and defer backend/database.

should_fix_issues.title: Approval threshold conflict is identified but not resolved
should_fix_issues.detail: The story correctly identifies conflicting governance thresholds between TASKS_FINANCE.md and PRD_SPEC_TASKLIST.md, but leaves implementation policy open.
should_fix_issues.recommendation: Resolve as PO/architecture clarification or isolate visual display thresholds from executable approval policy with an explicit acceptance criterion.

should_fix_issues.title: Testing scope is too broad to verify as one final gate
should_fix_issues.detail: The test plan spans migrations, RLS, APIs, workers, frontend components, security, reports, exports, and browser flows. This is valuable but too large for one story handoff.
should_fix_issues.recommendation: Move relevant tests into each split story so completion can be binary.

findings: Referenced Finance, global, architecture, and previous story documents are present in the repo.
Most technical claims trace to fbr-portal-docs/03_FINANCE, fbr-portal-docs/00_GERAL, docs/architecture, or prior stories.
No unsupported new library choice was detected in the story body; BullMQ, FastAPI, Redis, Supabase, Recharts, Next.js, Vitest, Testing Library, and Playwright are document-backed.
The implementation path adaptation from apps/modules/finance to apps/portal is an explicit inference from current repo architecture, not a direct product-doc requirement.
core-config.yaml has no enabled CodeRabbit integration.
Story includes the required disabled CodeRabbit skip notice.

handoff.condition: Story rejected as NO-GO; needs rework/splitting before Ready.
